source_pr.title: expression: fix IN expr critical bug
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: guo-shaoge <shaoge1994@163.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #25591 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: The implementation of **IN expr** will change its arguments array unexpectedly. For example:

```
t1.c1 IN (1, t2.c1, t2.c2);
original args: (t1.c1, 1, t2.c1, t2.c2)
after: (t1.c1, t2.c1, t2.c2, t2.c2)
```

### What is changed and how it works?
What's Changed: If we need to change local args slice, make a new underlying array instead of changing the expr's(`b.args` should not be changed)


### Related changes

- PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->


- Integration test


Side effects: May use extra memory to store local arguments array.

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- expression: fix IN expr critical bug

symptoms: rows := tk.MustQuery("select t1.col1, t2.col1, t2.col2 from t1_1 t1 inner join t2_1 t2 on t1.col1 not in (1,t2.col1,t2.col2) order by 1,2,3;") (expression/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: rows := tk.MustQuery("select t1.col1, t2.col1, t2.col2 from t1_1 t1 inner join t2_1 t2 on t1.col1 not in (1,t2.col1,t2.col2) order by 1,2,3;") (expression/integration_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: rows := tk.MustQuery("select t1.col1, t2.col1, t2.col2 from t1_1 t1 inner join t2_1 t2 on t1.col1 not in (1,t2.col1,t2.col2) order by 1,2,3;") (expression/integration_test.go).
fix_pattern: rows := tk.MustQuery("select t1.col1, t2.col1, t2.col2 from t1_1 t1 inner join t2_1 t2 on t1.col1 not in (1,t2.col1,t2.col2) order by 1,2,3;") (expression/integration_test.go)

evidence: evidence: rows := tk.MustQuery("select t1.col1, t2.col1, t2.col2 from t1_1 t1 inner join t2_1 t2 on t1.col1 not in (1,t2.col1,t2.col2) order by 1,2,3;") (expression/integration_test.go)
